title: BLUELIGHT: Source x Process Breakdown
description: Sunburst showing BLUELIGHT detection volume nested by Log Source and process. Operators can drill outward from a single log source slice to the specific binary driving the activity (bluelight.exe, iexplore.exe child shells, powershell loaders).
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Event Security Logs') and ('Process Name' like '%bluelight.exe' or 'Source Process' like '%bluelight.exe' or 'Parent Process Name' like '%iexplore.exe' or 'Destination Hostname' like '%graph.microsoft.com%' or 'Destination Hostname' like '%mail.google.com%' or 'Destination Hostname' like '%jquery.services%' or 'Granted Access' = '0x1fffff' or 'Target Filename' like '%bluelight.pdb%' or 'Target Filename' like '%cheV01%' or 'Command Line' like '%FromBase64String%' or 'Command Line' like '%cookie_name: OSID%' or 'Command Line' like '%ipinfo.io%') | stats count as Events by 'Log Source', 'Process Name' | sort -Events

ask_ai_prompts: Which log source contributes the most BLUELIGHT detections?
Drill into Sysmon Operational and show the top processes.
